title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: bind9.18 bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for bind9.18 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: BIND (Berkeley Internet Name Domain) is an implementation of the DNS (Domain Name System) protocols. BIND includes a DNS server (named), which resolves host names to IP addresses; a resolver library (routines for applications to use when interfacing with DNS); and tools for verifying that the DNS server is operating properly.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): bind9.18: libdns crash: lib/dns/name.c:667: REQUIRE((name1->attributes & 0x00000001) == (name2->attributes & 0x00000001)) failed [rhel-9] (JIRA:RHEL-96648)

full_text:
An update for bind9.18 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

BIND (Berkeley Internet Name Domain) is an implementation of the DNS (Domain Name System) protocols. BIND includes a DNS server (named), which resolves host names to IP addresses; a resolver library (routines for applications to use when interfacing with DNS); and tools for verifying that the DNS server is operating properly.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): bind9.18: libdns crash: lib/dns/name.c:667: REQUIRE((name1->attributes & 0x00000001) == (name2->attributes & 0x00000001)) failed [rhel-9] (JIRA:RHEL-96648)
